blocks.content: Saved queries lets you save a query for later and pull those queries back into the editor. This guide helps you both save and reintroduce saved queries. These can also be used in other features, like the Panel editor!
To save the query you're working on, complete these steps:
Confirm a query exists in the editor
blocks.tooltip: The save option only appears once a query has a data source, so make sure you have a query first.

blocks.content: Open the **Saved queries** menu, then choose **Save query**. In the dialog that opens, give your query a name (and an optional description and tags), then confirm to save it to the library.

steps.reftarget: div[data-testid='data-testid Query editor row'] button[data-testid='data-testid saved queries button']
steps.description: Click to open the **Saved queries** menu.

steps.reftarget: div[data-testid='data-testid portal-container'] button[data-testid='data-testid save to query library button']
steps.description: Click **Save query**, then complete the dialog to finish saving.

blocks.content: That's it - your query is now saved to the library and ready to reuse.
Prefer to pull in a query you saved earlier? You can either open a query in a new Query editor row or replace an existing row:

blocks.title: Reuse a saved query in a new row

blocks.content: Click **Add from saved queries** to open the Saved queries dialog. In the dialog, search or filter for the query you want, then select it to add it as a new row.

steps.reftarget: div[data-testid='data-testid explorer scroll view'] button[data-testid='data-testid explore add from query library button']
steps.description: Click **Add from saved queries**, then pick a query in the dialog.

blocks.content: The saved query now runs in its own new row, alongside your existing queries.
To swap out the query in a row you're already using, replace it instead:

blocks.title: Reuse a saved query in an existing row

blocks.content: In the row you want to change, open the **Saved queries** menu and choose **Replace query**. In the dialog, search or filter for the query you want, then select it to replace the current query.

steps.reftarget: div[data-testid='data-testid Query editor row'] button[data-testid='data-testid saved queries button']
steps.description: Click to open the **Saved queries** menu.

steps.reftarget: div[data-testid='data-testid portal-container'] button[data-testid='data-testid replace query from library button']
steps.description: Click **Replace query**, then pick a query in the dialog.

blocks.content: The row now runs your chosen saved query. You've saved and reused queries in Explore!
